name: San Diego
description: San Diego, California — history, geography, culture, and a tuna-fishing legacy.

questions.q: San Diego is the second-most populous city in which U.S. state?

source.notes: After Los Angeles.

questions.q: San Diego sits just north of which major Mexican city across the U.S. border?

source.notes: San Ysidro Port of Entry connects the two.

questions.q: Which world-famous zoo calls Balboa Park in San Diego its home?

source.notes: Grew out of the 1915 Panama-California Exposition.

questions.q: Which Navy-centered body of water sits at the heart of the city?

source.notes: Major U.S. Navy installation.

questions.q: Which NFL team called San Diego home before relocating to Los Angeles in 2017?

source.notes: Founded 1960 as the Los Angeles Chargers; San Diego 1961-2016.

questions.q: Which public research university in San Diego is part of the University of California system?

source.notes: Founded 1960 in La Jolla.

questions.q: Which MLB team plays its home games at Petco Park in downtown San Diego?

source.notes: San Diego Padres, NL West.

questions.q: Which Spanish explorer first claimed San Diego Bay for Spain in 1542?

source.notes: Portuguese-born explorer in Spanish service.

questions.q: The first of California's 21 Spanish missions was founded at San Diego in what year?

source.notes: Mission San Diego de Alcalá, founded by Junípero Serra.

questions.q: Which treaty ceded San Diego, along with the rest of California, from Mexico to the United States?

source.notes: Ended the Mexican-American War, 1848.

questions.q: What is the highest point within the San Diego city limits?

source.notes: Inside Mission Trails Regional Park, roughly 1,592 ft.

questions.q: Which large urban park, home to many museums and the zoo, grew out of expositions held in 1915 and 1935?

source.notes: 1,200+ acres; Panama-California Exposition, then California Pacific International Exposition.

questions.q: Which San Diego–Tijuana border crossing is often the busiest land border crossing in the Western Hemisphere?

source.original_a: The San Ysidro Port of Entry
source.notes: Handles tens of millions of crossings per year.

questions.q: What is the city of San Diego's Latin motto, which translates to 'Always Vigilant'?

source.notes: Adopted due to the city's long naval and military presence.

questions.q: During much of the 20th century, San Diego was known as what 'Capital of the World'?

source.notes: Tuna fleet peaked mid-century; Bumble Bee and Chicken of the Sea were locally based.

questions.q: Which beach community, just north of downtown San Diego, is home to the Scripps Institution of Oceanography?

source.notes: Scripps — part of UC San Diego — is in La Jolla.

questions.q: The late-spring and early-summer coastal fog over San Diego is colloquially known by which pair of nicknames?

source.notes: Result of the marine layer.

questions.q: Which famous 19th-century San Diego hotel is said to have inspired the title castle of The Wizard of Oz author L. Frank Baum's later novels?

source.notes: The 'Hotel Del,' opened 1888 on Coronado.

questions.q: Which SR-163 highway park and canyon divides Balboa Park, often cited as America's oldest 'freeway in a park'?

source.notes: Cabrillo Freeway through Cabrillo Canyon.

questions.q: In October 2003, a devastating wildfire burned a significant portion of San Diego County. What was it called?

source.notes: Then the largest wildfire in California history at the time.

questions.q: What rare pine species, native to the cliffs north of La Jolla, gave its name to a state natural reserve there?

source.notes: Pinus torreyana, one of the rarest pines in the U.S.

questions.q: Which 1986 Tom Cruise blockbuster opens with Navy F-14s flying over San Diego and its bay?

source.notes: Largely filmed at NAS Miramar, San Diego.
